description: System invariants that must hold across the governance system. Each invariant has an MBQL check that can be executed to verify compliance.

invariants.title: Natural language queries must be translatable to MBQL
invariants.description: Every operator-facing query interface must accept natural language input and translate it through the INTENT_IR pipeline before MBQL execution. Direct MBQL is permitted for programmatic access but NLQ must always be available as an alternative entry point.
invariants.rationale: Non-technical operators must be able to query the governance system without learning MBQL syntax. If the NLQ translator is bypassed, the system becomes inaccessible to its primary audience.
invariants.verification_method: Confirm that the NLQ translator pipeline exists (MBQL_v1.md Section 2), INTENT_IR schema exists (INTENT_IR.schema.json), and all 9 intent types have translation rules defined.

invariants.title: Bundle internal events must be recognized as evidence by SEE
invariants.description: The SEE Engine must recognize BUNDLE_INTERNAL_EVENTS as a first-class evidence source type ranked at DIRECT_OBSERVATION quality. Learning events (EVT_LEARNING_*) and failure events (EVT_FAIL_*) from inside bundles constitute the highest-quality evidence for claims about bundle behavior, failure history, and structural fixes.
invariants.rationale: Bundles self-report what failed, what changed, and what was fixed. This evidence is machine-emitted, timestamped, and tied to specific RCA reports. If SEE ignores bundle internal events, it loses access to its highest-quality evidence source and must rely on weaker inference from filenames.
invariants.mbql_check: FROM bundles WHERE category = 'os_bundle' SELECT filename, lifecycle_status
invariants.verification_method: Confirm that SEE_ENGINE_v1.md Section 1.10 defines BUNDLE_INTERNAL_EVENTS source type, that it is ranked DIRECT_OBSERVATION in the quality table, and that LEARNING_EVENT.schema.json exists with all 13 event types.

invariants.title: Every governance artifact must progress through DRAFT → VALIDATED → ENFORCED
invariants.description: Every artifact in the governance system must be registered in ARTIFACT_MATURITY.json with a claimed maturity stage (DRAFT, VALIDATED, or ENFORCED). The claimed stage must be mechanically provable: DRAFT requires file existence, VALIDATED requires a schema or structural check that passes, ENFORCED requires a validator wired into the pre-commit gate. False maturity claims block commits.
invariants.rationale: Without this invariant, specifications accumulate without enforcement. The gap between what is claimed and what is checked grows silently. This invariant ensures that every artifact either admits it is unvalidated (DRAFT) or proves its claimed maturity with mechanical evidence.
invariants.verification_method: Run .codex/validators/run_maturity_gate.py. It checks: (1) every tracked artifact's claimed maturity is provable, (2) summary counts match actual counts, (3) no governance artifacts exist that are untracked.
